Label: Host city relocated
Reason: Mount Vesuvius eruption strained Italy's finances
Story: Rome was originally set to host but withdrew after the 1906 Vesuvius eruption drained funds needed for the Games; London stepped in as replacement host.

Label: World War I begins
Reason: Outbreak of WWI halted international sport
Story: The outbreak of WWI halted all international sporting competition and set the stage for the first cancelled modern Olympics.

Label: Berlin Games cancelled
Reason: VI Olympiad cancelled due to WWI
Story: World War I made hosting impossible; many athletes were serving in the military instead of competing. This was the first cancellation of the modern Olympic Games.

Label: World War I ends
Reason: Armistice ends WWI
Story: The Armistice ended WWI, allowing the Olympic movement to resume planning toward the 1920 Antwerp Games.

Label: Central Powers excluded
Countries_Involved: Germany, Austria, Hungary, Bulgaria, Ottoman Empire
Reason: Defeated WWI powers not invited to compete
Story: Following WWI, the defeated Central Powers were barred from competing. The exclusions reflected post-war political tensions rather than athletic performance.

Label: Nazi propaganda Games
Reason: Nazi regime used Games as propaganda showcase
Story: Nazi Germany used the Olympics to showcase its regime to the world. Jesse Owens' victories directly challenged Nazi racial ideology on the world stage.

Label: World War II begins
Reason: Outbreak of WWII halted international sport
Story: The outbreak of WWII halted international sport for the second time in Olympic history, leading to consecutive Games cancellations.

Label: Tokyo Games cancelled
Reason: XII Olympiad cancelled; originally reassigned to Helsinki, then cancelled
Story: Tokyo relinquished hosting due to war in Asia; Helsinki was named replacement host but WWII forced cancellation before the Games could occur.

Label: Sapporo Games cancelled
Reason: V Winter Olympiad cancelled due to WWII
Story: The Winter Games scheduled for Sapporo were cancelled as WWII escalated across Asia and Europe.

Label: London Games cancelled
Reason: XIII Olympiad cancelled due to WWII
Story: As WWII continued, the Olympics were cancelled for a second consecutive edition, marking the longest interruption in Olympic history.

Label: Cortina d'Ampezzo Games cancelled
Reason: V Winter Olympiad (rescheduled) cancelled due to WWII
Story: The rescheduled Winter Games were cancelled again as WWII persisted across Europe.

Label: World War II ends
Reason: End of WWII; Olympics resume in 1948
Story: The end of WWII allowed the Olympic movement to resume, with the Games returning in London in 1948 after a 12-year gap.

Label: Germany & Japan excluded
Reason: Excluded due to wartime roles
Story: The first post-war Games were held under severe austerity. Germany and Japan were excluded because of their roles in WWII.

Label: Suez Crisis boycott
Reason: Protested the Suez Crisis
Story: Egypt, Iraq and Lebanon withdrew from Melbourne in protest of the Suez Crisis, marking one of the first major Olympic boycotts.

Label: Hungary invasion boycott
Reason: Protested Soviet invasion of Hungary
Story: The Soviet invasion of Hungary triggered boycotts by several European nations, and produced the famous 'Blood in the Water' water polo match.

Label: China-Taiwan withdrawal
Reason: Withdrew after Taiwan allowed to compete as Republic of China
Story: The People's Republic of China withdrew after Taiwan was permitted to compete, a recognition dispute that continued for decades.

Label: GANEFO boycott
Reason: Athletes barred over rival GANEFO Games participation
Story: Indonesia and North Korea withdrew after some of their athletes were barred from competing due to participation in the rival GANEFO Games.

Label: South Africa suspended
Reason: Suspended over apartheid policies
Story: South Africa was suspended over its apartheid policies, reflecting growing international opposition to racial segregation in sport. The ban lasted until apartheid ended.

Label: Tlatelolco massacre
Reason: Military crackdown on student protests before Games
Story: Ten days before the opening ceremony, a deadly military crackdown on student protests drew worldwide criticism, though the Games proceeded as scheduled.

Label: Black Power salute
Reason: Athletes protested racial inequality on podium
Story: Tommie Smith and John Carlos raised fists during the medal ceremony to protest racial inequality, one of the most iconic acts of athlete activism in Olympic history.

Label: Munich massacre
Reason: Israeli athletes taken hostage and killed
Story: Black September militants took the Israeli team hostage; 11 Israelis and 1 police officer were killed. The tragedy permanently transformed Olympic security planning.

Label: Rhodesia excluded
Reason: IOC withdrew invitation after protests
Story: Rhodesia's invitation was withdrawn after African-nation protests over its white-minority rule.

Label: African boycott
Reason: Protested New Zealand's rugby ties to apartheid South Africa
Story: African nations boycotted after the IOC declined to ban New Zealand over sporting ties with apartheid South Africa, one of the largest coordinated Olympic boycotts.

Label: Taiwan withdrawal
Reason: Naming/flag dispute with host nation
Story: Taiwan withdrew separately from the African boycott over a naming and flag dispute with the host nation.

Label: US-led boycott
Reason: Protested Soviet invasion of Afghanistan
Story: More than sixty nations, led by the US, boycotted in response to the Soviet invasion of Afghanistan, the largest boycott in Olympic history.

Label: Neutral flag athletes
Reason: Athletes competed under Olympic flag instead of full boycott
Story: Some countries allowed athletes to compete as neutrals under the Olympic flag rather than fully boycotting, distancing governments from the host nation.

Label: Soviet-led boycott
Reason: Retaliation for 1980 boycott
Story: The Soviet Union and most Eastern Bloc nations boycotted in apparent retaliation for the 1980 US-led boycott, sidelining many defending champions.

Label: North Korea boycott
Reason: Denied co-host status
Story: North Korea withdrew after negotiations for co-host status failed; a few allied nations joined in solidarity, though the impact was more limited than earlier boycotts.

Label: USSR & Yugoslavia breakup
Reason: New teams formed amid dissolution
Story: The dissolution of the Soviet Union led most former republics to compete as a Unified Team, while sanctions affected Yugoslav participation, reflecting dramatic geopolitical change.

Label: Centennial Park bombing
Reason: Domestic terrorist bombing during Games
Story: A bomb exploded in Atlanta's Olympic Park, killing two people and injuring over a hundred others, reinforcing the need for stronger Olympic security.

Label: Post-9/11 security
Reason: First Games after September 11 attacks
Story: Athens became the first Summer Games held after 9/11, with massive investment in surveillance and military deployment setting a new security benchmark.

Label: Tibet protests
Reason: Protests accompanied torch relay and Games
Story: Protests related to Tibet accompanied the torch relay and the Games, drawing international attention to China's human rights record without affecting competition.

Label: Terrorism preparedness
Reason: Large-scale security operation
Story: London ran one of the largest security operations in Olympic history, involving military personnel and advanced surveillance, with no major incidents occurring.

Label: Zika virus outbreak
Reason: Health concerns prompted athlete withdrawals
Story: Concerns over the Zika virus prompted a number of high-profile athlete withdrawals, particularly in golf and tennis, though the Games proceeded successfully.
